events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`, treating the missing kick-out flashing as an in-progress sequence stop because WRB/flashing integration depends on correcting that detail before covering or continuing adjacent finish work.

    \"inspect-water-shedding-continuity-before-continuing\"
  ],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Kick-out flashing is missing at the roof-wall termination.\",
    \"WRB and flashing sequence cannot be completed correctly until the kick-out is installed and lapped into the water-resistive barrier.\",
    \"Continuing siding, trim, cladding, or WRB close-in would conceal a known drainage defect and leave a high water-intrusion risk.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop related cladding, trim, WRB close-in, and finish installation at the roof-wall area.\",
    \"Expose the roof-wall termination enough to install the required kick-out flashing without reverse laps.\",
    \"Install kick-out flashing at the roof-wall intersection and integrate it with step flashing, WRB, siding clearance, and drainage plane in shingle-lap sequence.\",
    \"Verify the completed flashing/WRB integration against IRC R703 and the manufacturer instructions before work continues.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Missing kick-out flashing at a roof-wall intersection is an installed-defective and non-compliant condition. The flashing must be installed before WRB and cladding are closed in so water is directed out and away from the wall assembly rather than behind the siding or WRB.\",
  \"references\": [\"IRC R703 water-resistive barrier and flashing requirements\", \"Applicable manufacturer installation instructions for roof-wall flashing and WRB integration\"]
}' > /app/answer.json && jq . /app/answer.json"
